title: lathyrus laevigatus subsp. tournefortii
intro: Lathyrus laevigatus subsp. tournefortii, also known as the Tournefort's grass pea or annual vetchling, is a perennial plant in the pea family that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to help lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of menopause, due to its high levels of isoflavones, which act as phytoestrogens. The health benefits of Lathyrus laevigatus subsp. tournefortii include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve bone health, and alleviate symptoms of osteoporosis, making it a valuable addition to herbal medicine. Lathyrus laevigatus subsp. tournefortii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
